title: Customer Success Manager
location: London (Camden; onsite at customer depots 3d/wk + field)
key_evidence: Requires 3+ yrs (Ryan ~4.4, clears) AND 'already using AI tools day-to-day' — Ryan's Claude Code work is a direct match on the AI requirement.

Core of role = owning a portfolio of mid-market CS accounts end-to-end (onboarding/performance/renewal) + building the CS playbook. Ryan has community/investor-facing growth but NO B2B customer-success or account-portfolio track record — primary requirements gap.

Hard logistics requirement: comfortable with regular travel, early depot starts, onsite at customer depots 3 days/wk. Ryan's CV shows no field/operational customer-role appetite — a real fit/conversion risk he must genuinely want.

Domain = AI agents for industrial/construction/waste logistics (fleet routing). Outside Ryan's crypto/Web3 + AI-tooling domain; nice-to-have 'operational/field-based customer roles' and 'industrial sector interest' are gaps.

Stage/screening: YC Spring 2026, 5-person team, 12 customers, ~$1M ARR, founded 2025. Tiny founder-led team => founder-read screening and a founder interview in the loop (strong). Process: intro call -> take-home scenario -> founder interview.
Salary £50-90k + 0.10-0.25% equity + retention bonus — clears £50k floor comfortably (conversion-positive on comp).
Applying requires a YC/Work-at-a-Startup account; moderate applicant volume (YC visibility but niche field-CS role with off-putting depot requirement self-selects applicants down).
biggest_lever: Lean on the founder-read 5-person screening: a strong intro call + take-home where Ryan shows operator agency (build-the-playbook-from-zero, first-employee scaling) and AI fluency can offset the missing CS title.
biggest_risk: No CS/account-management background, no logistics/industrial domain, and a demanding depot-field/early-start requirement Ryan has never signalled appetite for — three stacked mismatches against a role built around field customer success.
apply_verdict: Apply only if genuinely open to field/depot work
availability: OPEN (confirmed 2026-06-09 — CSM still listed/accepting on YC jobs board; only open role shown for Dayjob)
estimation_method: Two corroborating signals (no exact date — YC-hosted, no Ashby/Lever). (1) Wayback CDX: Dayjob YC company page (ycombinator.com/companies/dayjob) FIRST captured 2026-04-09, refreshed 2026-05-03 => company/jobs presence existed by Apr 9 (posting floor). (2) JD copy states 'on track for $1M ARR by the end of May' in FUTURE tense => copy authored BEFORE end of May 2026. (3) Batch = YC P26 (Spring 2026), founded 2025. Combined: posting written ~April–late-May 2026 => ~4-8wk before today (2026-06-09). Midpoint early-mid May => ~6wk. Confirmed OPEN today via YC fetch.
recency_note: OPEN today (verified on YC jobs board). Dated via TWO signals: Wayback first-capture of YC company page 2026-04-09 (floor) + JD '$1M ARR by end of May' future-tense (written before end-May). => ~4-8wk old, midpoint ~6wk -> G_recency=32, med confidence. Was previously G=45 contextual-only; now anchored by Wayback floor. Callback_v2 52 -> 49.
momentum_signals: POSITIVE TRACTION: ARR grew from $496K to $653K in 7 months since launching sales Aug 2025; one customer cited £800K first-year revenue uplift; 8%+ efficiency gains from day one (YC company page, 2026-06-09). Healthy growth for a 5-person seed YC P26 startup.
CAUTION: JD claimed '$1M ARR by end of May' (future tense) but actual is $653K — the milestone was MISSED/overstated; tempers urgency framing slightly though trajectory is still up.

FOCUSED HIRING: exactly 1 open role company-wide — this CSM seat — confirmed OPEN today on YC jobs board. Single clean opening (no churn/repost), consistent with a tiny founder-led team making its first CS hire (high founder-read).
NEUTRAL: very small footprint (5 ppl, founded 2025) means thin public social/news cadence; YC Spring 2026 batch membership is the main external validation. No negative signals.
Source: WebFetch YC company page + WebSearch, 2026-06-09.
operator_fit_note: NO - despite 'build the CS playbook from zero', the core work is owning a B2B customer-success account portfolio + field/depot visits 3d/wk; this is CS/account-management in disguise, not operator/FA/generalist work. Only the AI-tools requirement lightly touches his spikes.
